items.title: The Silicon Valley congressional race is getting ugly
items.summary: The primary isn’t until early June, but the CA-17 race between five-term incumbent Ro Khanna and tech founder Ethan Agarwal is already getting nasty. Agarwal entered the race in March, backed by a roster of prominent tech billionaires largely in response to Khanna’s public support for a proposed California ballot measure that would impose a […]

items.title: Allbirds is selling for $39 million. It raised nearly 10 times that amount in its IPO.
items.summary: The collapse of the brand, which was originally venture-backed and that went public in 2021,  has been well-documented.

items.title: 15% of Americans say they’d be willing to work for an AI boss, according to new poll
items.summary: According to a Quinnipiac University poll, 15% of Americans say they'd be willing to have a job where their direct supervisor was an AI program that assigned tasks and set schedules.

items.title: Popular AI gateway startup LiteLLM ditches controversial startup Delve
items.summary: LiteLLM had obtained two security compliance certifications via Delve and fell victim to some horrific credential-stealing malware last week.

items.title: Former Coatue partner raises huge $65M seed for enterprise AI agent startup
items.summary: A few things turned investors' heads and drew them to participate in such a big round out of the gate.

items.title: As more Americans adopt AI tools, fewer say they can trust the results
items.summary: AI adoption is rising in the U.S., but trust remains low, with most Americans concerned about transparency, regulation, and the technology’s broader societal impact, according to a new Quinnipiac poll.

items.title: What we’re looking for in Startup Battlefield 2026 and how to put your best application forward
items.summary: Here's what we're looking for in your Startup Battlefield 200 application and how to make sure your application reflects it.

items.title: Delve whistleblower strikes again, with alleged receipts about ‘fake compliance’
items.summary: Shortly after Delve's founder published a lengthy denial of allegations, while promising changes, the accuser came forward with more alleged details.

items.title: Meta starts testing a premium subscription on Instagram
items.summary: Among the premium features on Instagram is the ability to view a Story without the poster seeing that you viewed it and the option to see how many people have rewatched your Story.

items.title: Bluesky’s new AI tool Attie is already the most blocked account other than J. D. Vance
items.summary: More than 125,000 Bluesky users have blocked Attie in just a few days,  sending a strong message from the user base.

items.title: Apple will hide your email address from apps and websites, but not cops
items.summary: Demands for Apple customer records by federal agents in recent months underscore the privacy limitations of email.

items.title: Uber is buying Berlin startup Blacklane to bolster its ‘Elite’ offering
items.summary: It's a notable exit for Blacklane, which has raised more than $100 million from backers like Mercedes-Benz and Sixt.

items.title: Mantis Biotech is making ‘digital twins’ of humans to help solve medicine’s data availability problem
items.summary: Mantis takes disparate sources of data to make synthetic datasets that can be used to build so-called "digital twins" of the human body, representing anatomy, physiology and behavior.

items.title: ScaleOps raises $130M to improve computing efficiency amid AI demand
items.summary: ScaleOps just raised $130M to tackle GPU shortages and soaring AI cloud costs by automating infrastructure in real time.

items.title: AI chip startup Rebellions raises $400 million at $2.3B valuation in pre-IPO round
items.summary: The startup, which is planning to go public later this year, designs chips specifically for AI inference, another challenger to Nvidia's dominance.

items.title: Mistral AI raises $830M in debt to set up a data center near Paris
items.summary: Mistral aims to start operating the data center by the second quarter of 2026.

items.title: Qodo raises $70M for code verification as AI coding scales
items.summary: As AI floods software development with code, Qodo is betting the real challenge is making sure it actually works.

items.title: Starcloud raises $170 million Series A to build data centers in space
items.summary: Starcloud becomes the fastest Y Combinator startup to reach unicorn status, just 17 months after demo day.

items.title: Why OpenAI really shut down Sora
items.summary: OpenAI's decision last week to shut down Sora, its AI video-generation tool, just six months after releasing it to the public, raised immediate suspicions. The app had invited users to upload their own faces — so was this some kind of elaborate data grab?

items.title: The Pixel 10a doesn’t have a camera bump, and it’s great
items.summary: The Google Pixel 10a can lay flat on a table, but otherwise the company hasn't brought a ton of upgrades to its newest budget smartphone.
